description_long: This object is a vintage-style record player with a large, faceted golden horn speaker mounted on a dark, rectangular wooden base. The base features subtle vertical striping, suggesting a classic wood grain finish, and supports a black, polygonal turntable platter with a small red square at its center, likely representing a record label. The horn is angular and geometric, flaring outward dramatically and attached to the base by a slender, angled support. A short, black tonearm is visible, positioned over the platter, with a small, distinct stylus at its end. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century gramophones, blending stylized, low-poly geometry with recognizable historical features. No dust cover or visible control knobs are present, giving the object a clean, minimalist appearance.
description: A vintage record player with a large golden horn, dark wooden base, and polygonal black turntable platter. The design is stylized and minimalist, evoking early gramophones.
description_view_0: The golden horn is prominently angled to the left, with the black platter and red center visible atop the striped wooden base.
description_view_1: The horn faces directly forward, showing the full opening and the symmetrical shape of the base and platter.
description_view_2: The horn is angled to the right, with the platter and red center clearly visible, and the base's wood grain pattern apparent.
description_view_3: The horn is angled toward the back, with the platter and tonearm more visible from the side, highlighting the geometric horn structure.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, polygonal in shape, and black in color with a small red square at its center. It occupies a moderate portion of the base's top surface and appears to be made of plastic or painted metal. The platter has a faceted, low-poly design with no visible grooves or detailed textures.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, short, and black, positioned over the platter. It is angular and appears to be made of metal or plastic, with a simple, stylized form and no visible joints or mechanisms.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible as a small, distinct component at the end of the tonearm, appearing as a tiny protrusion. It is likely made of metal or plastic and is attached to the tonearm.
